scoring_method: Each artifact scored on potential impact on the 8 forecasting targets {sla_safe_goodput_per_dollar, ttft, queue, cache, migration, cold_start, autoscaling, memory_pressure}. Class High/Medium/Low/No-Value reflects REALIZABLE incremental alpha given the repo's binding KPI and v1 state — NOT raw signal richness. An artifact that merely restates an already-ingested signal scores Low; one that unblocks a v1 caveat scores High. No percentage savings are claimed (docs/RESULTS.md gate).
kpi: sla_safe_goodput_per_dollar = (output_tokens if sla_met else 0) / (gpu_cost + energy_cost + migration_cost + cold_start_cost - cache_value)

artifact_scores.rationale: The single most valuable find. Provides a SECOND independent, production-derived prefix-reuse dataset (hash_ids, block_size=512) to cross-validate the cache_reuse_pct model that ML Alpha v1 flagged as shadow-ready BUT single-dataset (SwissAI only). Directly attacks the explicit v1 cross-dataset generalization caveat — the one public-data action that can promote a caveated shadow-ready model toward validation. Also a clean cache-hit-rate SIMULATION substrate.
artifact_scores.caveat: Workload trace, not operational telemetry: no measured latency/queue/cold-start. Cache-hit is simulated from reuse structure, not a measured per-request label.

artifact_scores.rationale: Richest MEASURED cold-start dataset in existence, with component decomposition (podAllocation/deployCode/deployDependency/scheduling) that is a structural template for the cold_start cost model in the Economic ML Alpha v1 sensitivity sweep, and a real cold-start DISTRIBUTION to calibrate a prior. num_pods is a warm-pool/autoscaling proxy.
artifact_scores.caveat: FaaS/CPU, not GPU model-load. Must be labeled cross-domain calibration-only — it CANNOT serve as server-class model_load_duration_s ground truth (the exact v1 pilot gap). Scores Medium not High because it improves the prior/structure but does not unblock the GPU headline.

artifact_scores.rationale: An actual GPU-INFERENCE serving fleet trace (7,386 GPU instances) with per-instance lifecycle (create/schedule/delete) from which scale-up/down events are derivable. The closest public artifact to inference autoscaling, and a natural extension of the in-repo Alibaba GPU v2020/v2023 lineage.
artifact_scores.caveat: Instance-level, not per-request; no queue/cache/cold-start/migration; autoscaling is INFERRED from lifecycle timestamps, not labeled.

artifact_scores.rationale: Best public preemption/queue/failure EVENT proxies (QUEUE, EVICT, FAIL/KILL). But job/task-level and non-GPU — the same proxy class v1 already credited to Google/Borg. No new economic-alpha unlock beyond what v1 catalogued.
artifact_scores.caveat: Not LLM-serving; EVICT is preemption not request migration; tail is CPU-util not request latency.

artifact_scores.rationale: Genuine per-call response-time + call-graph routing topology — the closest public per-request LATENCY outside the in-repo CARA/Optimum corpora. Could prior a routing-instability proxy, but no failure/queue-depth/cold-start labels and not LLM.
artifact_scores.caveat: Microservice RPC latency, not LLM TTFT; v2022 paper is about autoscaling but ships no scale-event labels.

artifact_scores.rationale: Clean per-invocation measured cold-start ms by runtime/memory/arch — a tidy FaaS cold-start distribution. Useful only as a weak cross-domain prior; does not represent GPU weight-load.
artifact_scores.caveat: CPU FaaS synthetic benchmark, not GPU, not production.

artifact_scores.rationale: Measured cold/warm exec times across clouds (2020). Same FaaS-prior value as lambda-perf, older.
artifact_scores.caveat: CPU FaaS; summary CSVs; per-invocation granularity unconfirmed.

artifact_scores.rationale: The most on-topic MIGRATION artifact (live request+KV migration mechanism + reproducible experiments), but ships only aggregated history logs (P99/Mean), not a per-migration event dataset. Reproducible-but-not-a-dataset.
artifact_scores.caveat: No raw per-migration records; migration cost can only be re-measured by re-running, which is pilot-equivalent.

artifact_scores.rationale: Richest SIGNAL CATEGORY coverage (queue/scheduling-delay, batch, replica MFU/memory, latency CDFs) but the outputs are SIMULATED — training on simulator output would learn the simulator, not reality (same trap v1 flagged for injected-constant latency).
artifact_scores.caveat: Simulator output; aggregate CDFs; not measured serving telemetry.

artifact_scores.rationale: Real LLM-serving WORKLOAD (arrival + tokens), already partially in-repo (azure_llm_2024). Drives replay but carries zero operational labels.
Raw blob R/W you can simulate a cache over (Faa$T methodology). A cache-hit SIMULATION substrate, but FaaS object cache not KV cache.
artifact_scores.caveat: No hit/miss label; not KV/prefix cache.

artifact_scores.rationale: Most relevant GPU model-load + live-migration SYSTEM, but CODE ONLY — no released measurements. High value as a system reference / pilot-instrumentation template; zero value as an ingestible dataset.
Production cold-start/autoscaling papers; measurements behind closed doors, figures only.
Only measured migration dataset, but VM-class (2017) and email-gated — not inference/model migration and not openly downloadable.
ZIP labeled 'Scripts and data' but contents unconfirmed; likely simulation + Azure-derived inputs.
artifact_scores.caveat: Requires download to verify; not credited until verified.

artifact_scores.class: High (as PILOT instrumentation map, not as data)
artifact_scores.rationale: Not datasets, but they define EXACTLY which frontier signals a pilot can capture off-the-shelf and under which metric names (Ray Serve replica_startup/multiplexed_model_load + autoscaling_*; vLLM prefix_cache_hits + kv_block_lifetime + num_preemptions; SGLang/LMCache cache_hit_rate; Triton GPU util + request_failure). This converts v1's vague 'blocked_by_pilot_telemetry' into a named, actionable PILOT_TELEMETRY_CONTRACT mapping. High value for the pilot roadmap; not ingestible public data.
artifact_scores.caveat: Runtime telemetry — must run the system; request MIGRATION has no metric anywhere (needs custom instrumentation).

per_signal_category_scoring.cache: High (Mooncake unblocks the v1 single-dataset caveat; live metrics measure it)
per_signal_category_scoring.ttft_tail_latency: Medium (measured latency exists in-repo + microservices; upstream-cause attribution still pilot)
per_signal_category_scoring.queue: Medium (job-level public proxies extend; serving-request-level still pilot, but now with named metrics)
per_signal_category_scoring.memory_pressure: Medium (peak_vram already shadow-ready; no new public data changes it; vLLM KV metrics aid pilot)
per_signal_category_scoring.cold_start: Medium-leverage / Low-realizable-public (FaaS priors only; GPU model-load measured-unreleased; Ray Serve metric makes it pilot-reconstructable)
per_signal_category_scoring.autoscaling: Medium-leverage / Low-realizable-public (proxies only; Ray Serve/Knative makes it pilot-reconstructable)
per_signal_category_scoring.migration: High-leverage / Very-Low-realizable (no inference migration data anywhere; the ONLY signal needing CUSTOM pilot instrumentation beyond standard metrics)
per_signal_category_scoring.sla_safe_goodput_per_dollar: unchanged as a target (deterministic given inputs per v1); alpha still routes through forecasting the upstream uncertain signals above
